     1→import { createHeadlessEditor } from '@lexical/headless'
     2→import { PortalThemeProvider } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
     3→import {
     4→  allNodes,
     5→  ColorSchemeProvider,
     6→  editorTheme,
     7→  FootnoteDefinitionsProvider,
     8→  getVariantClass,
     9→  NestedContentRendererProvider,
    10→  RendererConfigProvider,
    11→} from '@haklex/rich-editor/static'
    12→import type { SerializedEditorState } from 'lexical'
    13→import { $getRoot } from 'lexical'
    14→import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
    15→
    16→import { renderBuiltinNode } from './engine/renderBuiltinNode'
    17→import { renderTextNode } from './engine/renderTextNode'
    18→import { preprocessFootnotes } from './preprocess/footnote'
    19→import type { RichSSRRendererProps } from './types'
    20→
    21→function renderTree(
    22→  node: any,
    23→  decorations: Record<string, ReactNode>,
    24→  headingSlugs: Map<string, number>,
    25→  key: string,
    26→): ReactNode {
    27→  const nodeKey = node.getKey ? node.getKey() : key
    28→
    29→  if (decorations[nodeKey] !== undefined) {
    30→    return decorations[nodeKey]
    31→  }
    32→
    33→  const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}
    34→
    35→  if (serialized.type === 'text') {
    36→    return renderTextNode(serialized, nodeKey)
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  let children: ReactNode[] | null = null
    40→  if (node.getChildren) {
    41→    const childNodes = node.getChildren()
    42→    if (childNodes.length > 0) {
    43→      children = childNodes.map((child: any, i: number) =>
    44→        renderTree(child, decorations, headingSlugs, `${nodeKey}-${i}`),
    45→      )
    46→    }
    47→  }
    48→
    49→  return renderBuiltinNode(serialized, nodeKey, children, headingSlugs)
    50→}
    51→
    52→function renderEditorToReact(
    53→  value: SerializedEditorState,
    54→  nodes: any[],
    55→): { content: ReactNode; footnoteData: ReturnType<typeof preprocessFootnotes>; renderNestedContent: (state: SerializedEditorState) => ReactNode } {
    56→  const editor = createHeadlessEditor({
    57→    nodes,
    58→    theme: editorTheme,
    59→    editable: false,
    60→    onError: (error: Error) => {
    61→      console.error('[RichSSRRenderer]', error)
    62→    },
    63→  })
    64→
    65→  const editorState = editor.parseEditorState(value)
    66→  editor.setEditorState(editorState)
    67→
    68→  const footnoteData = preprocessFootnotes(value)
    69→  const decorations = editor.getDecorators<ReactNode>()
    70→
    71→  let content: ReactNode = null
    72→  editorState.read(() => {
    73→    const root = $getRoot()
    74→    const headingSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
    75→    const children = root.getChildren().map((child: any, i: number) =>
    76→      renderTree(child, decorations, headingSlugs, `ssr-${i}`),
    77→    )
    78→    content = <>{children}</>
    79→  })
    80→
    81→  const renderNestedContent = (state: SerializedEditorState): ReactNode => {
    82→    const nestedEditor = createHeadlessEditor({
    83→      nodes,
    84→      theme: editorTheme,
    85→      editable: false,
    86→      onError: (error: Error) => {
    87→        console.error('[RichSSRRenderer:nested]', error)
    88→      },
    89→    })
    90→    const nestedState = nestedEditor.parseEditorState(state)
    91→    nestedEditor.setEditorState(nestedState)
    92→    const nestedDecorations = nestedEditor.getDecorators<ReactNode>()
    93→    let nested: ReactNode = null
    94→    nestedState.read(() => {
    95→      const root = $getRoot()
    96→      const headingSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
    97→      const ch = root.getChildren().map((child: any, i: number) =>
    98→        renderTree(child, nestedDecorations, headingSlugs, `nested-${i}`),
    99→      )
   100→      nested = <>{ch}</>
   101→    })
   102→    return nested
   103→  }
   104→
   105→  return { content, footnoteData, renderNestedContent }
   106→}
   107→
   108→export function RichSSRRenderer({
   109→  value,
   110→  variant = 'article',
   111→  theme = 'light',
   112→  className,
   113→  style,
   114→  as: Component = 'div',
   115→  rendererConfig,
   116→  extraNodes,
   117→}: RichSSRRendererProps) {
   118→  const variantClass = getVariantClass(variant, theme)
   119→  const nodes = extraNodes ? [...allNodes, ...extraNodes] : allNodes
   120→
   121→  const { content, footnoteData, renderNestedContent } = renderEditorToReact(value, nodes)
   122→
   123→  const classes = ['rich-content', variantClass, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')
   124→
   125→  return (
   126→    <PortalThemeProvider className={variantClass}>
   127→      <ColorSchemeProvider colorScheme={theme}>
   128→        <RendererConfigProvider
   129→          config={rendererConfig}
   130→          mode="renderer"
   131→          variant={variant}
   132→        >
   133→          <FootnoteDefinitionsProvider
   134→            definitions={footnoteData.definitions}
   135→            displayNumberMap={footnoteData.displayNumberMap}
   136→          >
   137→            <NestedContentRendererProvider value={renderNestedContent}>
   138→              <Component
   139→                className={classes}
   140→                style={style}
   141→                data-theme={theme}
   142→              >
   143→                {content}
   144→              </Component>

   146→          </FootnoteDefinitionsProvider>
   147→        </RendererConfigProvider>
   148→      </ColorSchemeProvider>
   149→    </PortalThemeProvider>
   150→  )
   151→}

     1→import type {

     9→} from 'lexical'
    10→import { DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
    11→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
    12→import { createElement } from 'react'
    13→
    14→import { AlertStaticDecorator } from '../components/renderers/AlertStaticDecorator'
    15→import { extractTextContent } from '../utils/extractTextContent'
    16→
    17→export type AlertType = 'note' | 'tip' | 'important' | 'warning' | 'caution'
    18→
    19→export const ALERT_TYPES: AlertType[] = [
    20→  'note',
    21→  'tip',
    22→  'important',
    23→  'warning',
    24→  'caution',
    25→]
    26→
    27→export const ALERT_LABELS: Record<AlertType, string> = {
    28→  note: 'Note',
    29→  tip: 'Tip',
    30→  important: 'Important',
    31→  warning: 'Warning',
    32→  caution: 'Caution',
    33→}
    34→
    35→export type SerializedAlertQuoteNode = Spread<
    36→  {
    37→    alertType: AlertType
    38→    content: SerializedEditorState
    39→  },
    40→  SerializedLexicalNode
    41→>
    42→
    43→export class AlertQuoteNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
    44→  __alertType: AlertType
    45→  __contentState: SerializedEditorState
    46→
    47→  static getType(): string {
    48→    return 'alert-quote'
    49→  }
    50→
    51→  static clone(node: AlertQuoteNode): AlertQuoteNode {
    52→    return new AlertQuoteNode(
    53→      node.__alertType,
    54→      node.__contentState,
    55→      node.__key,
    56→    )
    57→  }
    58→
    59→  constructor(
    60→    alertType: AlertType,
    61→    contentState?: SerializedEditorState,
    62→    key?: NodeKey,
    63→  ) {
    64→    super(key)
    65→    this.__alertType = alertType
    66→    this.__contentState = contentState || {
    67→      root: {
    68→        children: [
    69→          {
    70→            type: 'paragraph',
    71→            children: [],
    72→            direction: null,
    73→            format: '',
    74→            indent: 0,
    75→            textFormat: 0,
    76→            textStyle: '',
    77→            version: 1,
    78→          },
    79→        ],
    80→        direction: null,
    81→        format: '',
    82→        indent: 0,
    83→        type: 'root',
    84→        version: 1,
    85→      },
    86→    } as unknown as SerializedEditorState
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    90→    const div = document.createElement('div')
    91→    div.className = `rich-alert rich-alert-${this.__alertType}`
    92→    return div
    93→  }
    94→
    95→  updateDOM(prevNode: AlertQuoteNode, dom: HTMLElement): boolean {
    96→    if (prevNode.__alertType !== this.__alertType) {
    97→      dom.className = `rich-alert rich-alert-${this.__alertType}`
    98→    }
    99→    return false
   100→  }
   101→
   102→  isInline(): boolean {
   103→    return false
   104→  }
   105→
   106→  getAlertType(): AlertType {
   107→    return this.__alertType
   108→  }
   109→
   110→  setAlertType(alertType: AlertType): void {
   111→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   112→    writable.__alertType = alertType
   113→  }
   114→
   115→  getContentState(): SerializedEditorState {
   116→    return this.getLatest().__contentState
   117→  }
   118→
   119→  setContentState(state: SerializedEditorState): void {
   120→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   121→    writable.__contentState = state
   122→  }
   123→
   124→  getTextContent(): string {
   125→    return extractTextContent(this.__contentState)
   126→  }
   127→
   128→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedAlertQuoteNode): AlertQuoteNode {
   129→    return new AlertQuoteNode(serializedNode.alertType, serializedNode.content)
   130→  }
   131→
   132→  exportJSON(): SerializedAlertQuoteNode {
   133→    return {
   134→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   135→      type: 'alert-quote',
   136→      alertType: this.__alertType,
   137→      content: this.__contentState,
   138→      version: 1,
   139→    }
   140→  }
   141→
   142→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
   143→    return createElement(AlertStaticDecorator, {
   144→      alertType: this.__alertType,
   145→      contentState: this.__contentState,
   146→    })
   147→  }
   148→}
   149→
   150→export function $createAlertQuoteNode(
   151→  alertType: AlertType,
   152→  contentState?: SerializedEditorState,
   153→): AlertQuoteNode {
   154→  return new AlertQuoteNode(alertType, contentState)
   155→}
   156→
   157→export function $isAlertQuoteNode(
   158→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
   159→): node is AlertQuoteNode {
   160→  return node instanceof AlertQuoteNode
   161→}
   162→
```

```text
     1→import type { SerializedEditorState } from 'lexical'
     2→
     3→import type { AlertType } from '../../nodes/AlertQuoteNode'
     4→import { useNestedContentRenderer } from '../../context/NestedContentRendererContext'
     5→import { RendererWrapper } from '../RendererWrapper'
     6→import { AlertRenderer } from './AlertRenderer'
     7→
     8→interface AlertStaticDecoratorProps {
     9→  alertType: AlertType
    10→  contentState: SerializedEditorState
    11→}
    12→
    13→export function AlertStaticDecorator({
    14→  alertType,
    15→  contentState,
    16→}: AlertStaticDecoratorProps) {
    17→  const renderContent = useNestedContentRenderer()
    18→
    19→  return (
    20→    <>
    21→      <RendererWrapper
    22→        rendererKey="Alert"
    23→        defaultRenderer={AlertRenderer}
    24→        props={{ type: alertType }}
    25→      />
    26→      <div className="rich-alert-content">
    27→        {renderContent(contentState)}
    28→      </div>
    29→    </>
    30→  )
    31→}
    32→
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/BannerNode.ts`

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalEditor,
     4→  LexicalNode,
     5→  NodeKey,
     6→  SerializedEditorState,
     7→  SerializedLexicalNode,
     8→  Spread,
     9→} from 'lexical'
    10→import { DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
    11→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
    12→import { createElement } from 'react'
    13→
    14→import { BannerStaticDecorator } from '../components/renderers/BannerStaticDecorator'
    15→import { extractTextContent } from '../utils/extractTextContent'
    16→
    17→export type BannerType = 'note' | 'tip' | 'important' | 'warning' | 'caution'
    18→
    19→const LEGACY_TYPE_MAP: Record<string, BannerType> = {
    20→  info: 'note',
    21→  success: 'tip',
    22→  error: 'caution',
    23→}
    24→
    25→export function normalizeBannerType(type: string): BannerType {
    26→  if (type in LEGACY_TYPE_MAP) return LEGACY_TYPE_MAP[type]
    27→  return (type as BannerType) || 'note'
    28→}
    29→
    30→export const BANNER_TYPES: BannerType[] = [
    31→  'note',
    32→  'tip',
    33→  'important',
    34→  'warning',
    35→  'caution',
    36→]
    37→
    38→export const BANNER_LABELS: Record<BannerType, string> = {
    39→  note: 'Note',
    40→  tip: 'Tip',
    41→  important: 'Important',
    42→  warning: 'Warning',
    43→  caution: 'Caution',
    44→}
    45→
    46→export type SerializedBannerNode = Spread<
    47→  {
    48→    bannerType: BannerType
    49→    content: SerializedEditorState
    50→  },
    51→  SerializedLexicalNode
    52→>
    53→
    54→interface LegacySerializedBannerNode extends SerializedBannerNode {
    55→  children?: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    56→}
    57→
    58→export class BannerNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
    59→  __bannerType: BannerType
    60→  __contentState: SerializedEditorState
    61→
    62→  static getType(): string {
    63→    return 'banner'
    64→  }
    65→
    66→  static clone(node: BannerNode): BannerNode {
    67→    return new BannerNode(node.__bannerType, node.__contentState, node.__key)
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  constructor(
    71→    bannerType: BannerType,
    72→    contentState?: SerializedEditorState,
    73→    key?: NodeKey,
    74→  ) {
    75→    super(key)
    76→    this.__bannerType = bannerType
    77→    this.__contentState = contentState || {
    78→      root: {
    79→        children: [
    80→          {
    81→            type: 'paragraph',
    82→            children: [],
    83→            direction: null,
    84→            format: '',
    85→            indent: 0,
    86→            textFormat: 0,
    87→            textStyle: '',
    88→            version: 1,
    89→          },
    90→        ],
    91→        direction: null,
    92→        format: '',
    93→        indent: 0,
    94→        type: 'root',
    95→        version: 1,
    96→      },
    97→    } as unknown as SerializedEditorState
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
   101→    const div = document.createElement('div')
   102→    div.className = `rich-banner rich-banner-${this.__bannerType}`
   103→    return div
   104→  }
   105→
   106→  updateDOM(prevNode: BannerNode, dom: HTMLElement): boolean {
   107→    if (prevNode.__bannerType !== this.__bannerType) {
   108→      dom.className = `rich-banner rich-banner-${this.__bannerType}`
   109→    }
   110→    return false
   111→  }
   112→
   113→  isInline(): boolean {
   114→    return false
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  getBannerType(): BannerType {
   118→    return this.__bannerType
   119→  }
   120→
   121→  setBannerType(bannerType: BannerType): void {
   122→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   123→    writable.__bannerType = bannerType
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  getContentState(): SerializedEditorState {
   127→    return this.getLatest().__contentState
   128→  }
   129→
   130→  setContentState(state: SerializedEditorState): void {
   131→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   132→    writable.__contentState = state
   133→  }
   134→
   135→  getTextContent(): string {
   136→    return extractTextContent(this.__contentState)
   137→  }
   138→
   139→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedBannerNode): BannerNode {
   140→    const legacy = serializedNode as LegacySerializedBannerNode
   141→    const bannerType = normalizeBannerType(serializedNode.bannerType)
   142→
   143→    if (serializedNode.content) {
   144→      return new BannerNode(bannerType, serializedNode.content)
   145→    }
   146→
   147→    if (legacy.children) {
   148→      const content = {
   149→        root: {
   150→          children: legacy.children,
   151→          direction: null,
   152→          format: '',
   153→          indent: 0,
   154→          type: 'root',
   155→          version: 1,
   156→        },
   157→      } as unknown as SerializedEditorState
   158→      return new BannerNode(bannerType, content)
   159→    }
   160→
   161→    return new BannerNode(bannerType)
   162→  }
   163→
   164→  exportJSON(): SerializedBannerNode {
   165→    return {
   166→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   167→      type: 'banner',
   168→      bannerType: this.__bannerType,
   169→      content: this.__contentState,
   170→      version: 1,
   171→    }
   172→  }
   173→
   174→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
   175→    return createElement(BannerStaticDecorator, {
   176→      bannerType: this.__bannerType,
   177→      contentState: this.__contentState,
   178→    })
   179→  }
   180→}
   181→
   182→export function $createBannerNode(
   183→  bannerType: BannerType,
   184→  contentState?: SerializedEditorState,
   185→): BannerNode {
   186→  return new BannerNode(bannerType, contentState)
   187→}
   188→
   189→export function $isBannerNode(
   190→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
   191→): node is BannerNode {
   192→  return node instanceof BannerNode
   193→}
   194→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/GridContainerNode.ts`

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalEditor,
     4→  LexicalNode,
     5→  NodeKey,
     6→  SerializedEditorState,
     7→  SerializedLexicalNode,
     8→  Spread,
     9→} from 'lexical'
    10→import { DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
    11→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
    12→import { createElement } from 'react'
    13→
    14→import { GridStaticDecorator } from '../components/renderers/GridStaticDecorator'
    15→import { extractTextContent } from '../utils/extractTextContent'
    16→
    17→export type SerializedGridContainerNode = Spread<
    18→  {
    19→    cols: number
    20→    gap?: string
    21→    cells: SerializedEditorState[]
    22→  },
    23→  SerializedLexicalNode
    24→>
    25→
    26→interface LegacySerializedGridNode {
    27→  type?: string
    28→  version?: number
    29→  cols?: number
    30→  gap?: string | number
    31→  cells?: SerializedEditorState[]
    32→  children?: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    33→}
    34→
    35→export class GridContainerNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
    36→  __cols: number
    37→  __gap: string
    38→  __cellStates: SerializedEditorState[]
    39→
    40→  static getType(): string {
    41→    return 'grid-container'
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  static clone(node: GridContainerNode): GridContainerNode {
    45→    return new GridContainerNode(
    46→      node.__cols,
    47→      node.__gap,
    48→      [...node.__cellStates],
    49→      node.__key,
    50→    )
    51→  }
    52→
    53→  constructor(
    54→    cols = 2,
    55→    gap?: string,
    56→    cellStates?: SerializedEditorState[],
    57→    key?: NodeKey,
    58→  ) {
    59→    super(key)
    60→    this.__cols = cols
    61→    this.__gap = gap || '16px'
    62→    if (cellStates) {
    63→      this.__cellStates = cellStates
    64→    } else {
    65→      const emptyState = {
    66→        root: {
    67→          children: [
    68→            {
    69→              type: 'paragraph',
    70→              children: [],
    71→              direction: null,
    72→              format: '',
    73→              indent: 0,
    74→              textFormat: 0,
    75→              textStyle: '',
    76→              version: 1,
    77→            },
    78→          ],
    79→          direction: null,
    80→          format: '',
    81→          indent: 0,
    82→          type: 'root',
    83→          version: 1,
    84→        },
    85→      } as unknown as SerializedEditorState
    86→      this.__cellStates = Array.from({ length: cols }, () => emptyState)
    87→    }
    88→  }
    89→
    90→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    91→    const div = document.createElement('div')
    92→    div.className = 'rich-grid-container'
    93→    return div
    94→  }
    95→
    96→  updateDOM(): boolean {
    97→    return false
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  isInline(): boolean {
   101→    return false
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  getCols(): number {
   105→    return this.getLatest().__cols
   106→  }
   107→
   108→  setCols(cols: number): void {
   109→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   110→    const prev = writable.__cellStates.length
   111→    writable.__cols = cols
   112→    if (cols > prev) {
   113→      const emptyState = {
   114→        root: {
   115→          children: [
   116→            {
   117→              type: 'paragraph',
   118→              children: [],
   119→              direction: null,
   120→              format: '',
   121→              indent: 0,
   122→              textFormat: 0,
   123→              textStyle: '',
   124→              version: 1,
   125→            },
   126→          ],
   127→          direction: null,
   128→          format: '',
   129→          indent: 0,
   130→          type: 'root',
   131→          version: 1,
   132→        },
   133→      } as unknown as SerializedEditorState
   134→      for (let i = prev; i < cols; i++) {
   135→        writable.__cellStates.push(emptyState)
   136→      }
   137→    }
   138→  }
   139→
   140→  getGap(): string {
   141→    return this.getLatest().__gap
   142→  }
   143→
   144→  setGap(gap: string): void {
   145→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   146→    writable.__gap = gap
   147→  }
   148→
   149→  getCellStates(): SerializedEditorState[] {
   150→    return this.getLatest().__cellStates
   151→  }
   152→
   153→  addCells(count: number): void {
   154→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   155→    const emptyState = {
   156→      root: {
   157→        children: [
   158→          {
   159→            type: 'paragraph',
   160→            children: [],
   161→            direction: null,
   162→            format: '',
   163→            indent: 0,
   164→            textFormat: 0,
   165→            textStyle: '',
   166→            version: 1,
   167→          },
   168→        ],
   169→        direction: null,
   170→        format: '',
   171→        indent: 0,
   172→        type: 'root',
   173→        version: 1,
   174→      },
   175→    } as unknown as SerializedEditorState
   176→    for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {
   177→      writable.__cellStates.push(emptyState)
   178→    }
   179→  }
   180→
   181→  removeCells(count: number): void {
   182→    const writable = this.getWritable()
   183→    const states = writable.__cellStates
   184→    const toRemove = Math.min(count, states.length)
   185→    for (let i = 0; i < toRemove; i++) {
   186→      const state = states.at(-1)
   187→      if (!state) break
   188→      const isEmpty = extractTextContent(state).trim() === ''
   189→      if (!isEmpty) break
   190→      states.pop()
   191→    }
   192→  }
   193→
   194→  getTextContent(): string {
   195→    return this.__cellStates.map((s) => extractTextContent(s)).join('\n')
   196→  }
   197→
   198→  static importJSON(
   199→    serializedNode: SerializedGridContainerNode,
   200→  ): GridContainerNode {
   201→    const legacy = serializedNode as LegacySerializedGridNode
   202→    const cols = legacy.cols || 2
   203→    const rawGap = legacy.gap
   204→    const gap = typeof rawGap === 'number' ? `${rawGap}px` : rawGap
   205→
   206→    if (legacy.cells && legacy.cells.length > 0) {
   207→      return new GridContainerNode(cols, gap, legacy.cells)
   208→    }
   209→
   210→    if (legacy.children) {
   211→      const cellStates: SerializedEditorState[] = legacy.children.map(
   212→        (child) => {
   213→          return {
   214→            root: {
   215→              children: [child],
   216→              direction: null,
   217→              format: '',
   218→              indent: 0,
   219→              type: 'root',
   220→              version: 1,
   221→            },
   222→          } as unknown as SerializedEditorState
   223→        },
   224→      )
   225→      return new GridContainerNode(cols, gap, cellStates)
   226→    }
   227→
   228→    return new GridContainerNode(cols, gap)
   229→  }
   230→
   231→  exportJSON(): SerializedGridContainerNode {
   232→    return {
   233→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   234→      type: 'grid-container',
   235→      cols: this.__cols,
   236→      gap: this.__gap,
   237→      cells: this.__cellStates,
   238→      version: 1,
   239→    }
   240→  }
   241→
   242→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
   243→    return createElement(GridStaticDecorator, {
   244→      cols: this.__cols,
   245→      gap: this.__gap,
   246→      cellStates: this.__cellStates,
   247→    })
   248→  }
   249→}
   250→
   251→export function $createGridContainerNode(
   252→  cols = 2,
   253→  gap?: string,
   254→): GridContainerNode {
   255→  return new GridContainerNode(cols, gap)
   256→}
   257→
   258→export function $isGridContainerNode(
   259→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
   260→): node is GridContainerNode {
   261→  return node instanceof GridContainerNode
   262→}
   263→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/ImageNode.ts`

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalEditor,
     4→  LexicalNode,
     5→  NodeKey,
     6→  SerializedLexicalNode,
     7→  Spread,
     8→} from 'lexical'
     9→import { $insertNodes, DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
    10→import { ImageIcon } from 'lucide-react'
    11→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
    12→import { createElement } from 'react'
    13→
    14→import { ImageRenderer } from '../components/renderers/ImageRenderer'
    15→import { createRendererDecoration } from '../components/RendererWrapper'
    16→import type { SlashMenuItemConfig } from '../types/slash-menu'
    17→
    18→export type SerializedImageNode = Spread<
    19→  {
    20→    src: string
    21→    altText: string
    22→    width?: number
    23→    height?: number
    24→    caption?: string
    25→    thumbhash?: string
    26→    accent?: string
    27→  },
    28→  SerializedLexicalNode
    29→>
    30→
    31→function sanitizeImageSrc(src: string): string {
    32→  const trimmed = src.trim()
    33→  if (/^(javascript\s*:|vbscript\s*:|data\s*:(?!image\/))/i.test(trimmed)) {
    34→    return ''
    35→  }
    36→  return trimmed
    37→}
    38→
    39→function sanitizeColor(value: string | undefined): string | undefined {
    40→  if (!value) return undefined
    41→  const trimmed = value.trim()
    42→  if (/^#[\da-f]{3,8}$/i.test(trimmed)) return trimmed
    43→  if (/^(rgb|hsl)a?\([^)]+\)$/i.test(trimmed)) return trimmed
    44→  if (/^[a-z]{3,20}$/i.test(trimmed)) return trimmed
    45→  return undefined
    46→}
    47→
    48→export interface ImageNodePayload {
    49→  src: string
    50→  altText: string
    51→  width?: number
    52→  height?: number
    53→  caption?: string
    54→  thumbhash?: string
    55→  accent?: string
    56→}
    57→
    58→export class ImageNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
    59→  __src: string
    60→  __altText: string
    61→  __width?: number
    62→  __height?: number
    63→  __caption?: string
    64→  __thumbhash?: string
    65→  __accent?: string
    66→
    67→  static slashMenuItems: SlashMenuItemConfig[] = [
    68→    {
    69→      title: 'Image',
    70→      icon: createElement(ImageIcon, { size: 20 }),
    71→      description: 'Upload or embed an image',
    72→      keywords: ['image', 'picture', 'photo'],
    73→      section: 'MEDIA',
    74→      onSelect: (editor) => {
    75→        editor.update(() => {
    76→          $insertNodes([$createImageNode({ src: '', altText: '' })])
    77→        })
    78→      },
    79→    },
    80→  ]
    81→
    82→  static getType(): string {
    83→    return 'image'
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  static clone(node: ImageNode): ImageNode {
    87→    return new ImageNode(
    88→      {
    89→        src: node.__src,
    90→        altText: node.__altText,
    91→        width: node.__width,
    92→        height: node.__height,
    93→        caption: node.__caption,
    94→        thumbhash: node.__thumbhash,
    95→        accent: node.__accent,
    96→      },
    97→      node.__key,
    98→    )
    99→  }
   100→
   101→  constructor(payload: ImageNodePayload, key?: NodeKey) {
   102→    super(key)
   103→    this.__src = sanitizeImageSrc(payload.src)
   104→    this.__altText = payload.altText
   105→    this.__width = payload.width
   106→    this.__height = payload.height
   107→    this.__caption = payload.caption
   108→    this.__thumbhash = payload.thumbhash
   109→    this.__accent = sanitizeColor(payload.accent)
   110→  }
   111→
   112→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
   113→    const div = document.createElement('div')
   114→    div.className = 'rich-image-wrapper'
   115→    return div
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  updateDOM(): boolean {
   119→    return false
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  isInline(): boolean {
   123→    return false
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedImageNode): ImageNode {
   127→    return $createImageNode({
   128→      src: serializedNode.src,
   129→      altText: serializedNode.altText,
   130→      width: serializedNode.width,
   131→      height: serializedNode.height,
   132→      caption: serializedNode.caption,
   133→      thumbhash: serializedNode.thumbhash,
   134→      accent: serializedNode.accent,
   135→    })
   136→  }
   137→
   138→  exportJSON(): SerializedImageNode {
   139→    return {
   140→      ...super.exportJSON(),
   141→      type: 'image',
   142→      src: this.__src,
   143→      altText: this.__altText,
   144→      width: this.__width,
   145→      height: this.__height,
   146→      caption: this.__caption,
   147→      thumbhash: this.__thumbhash,
   148→      accent: this.__accent,
   149→      version: 1,
   150→    }
   151→  }
   152→
   153→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
   154→    return createRendererDecoration('Image', ImageRenderer, {
   155→      src: this.__src,
   156→      altText: this.__altText,
   157→      width: this.__width,
   158→      height: this.__height,
   159→      caption: this.__caption,
   160→      thumbhash: this.__thumbhash,
   161→      accent: this.__accent,
   162→    })
   163→  }
   164→}
   165→
   166→export function $createImageNode(payload: ImageNodePayload): ImageNode {
   167→  return new ImageNode(payload)
   168→}
   169→
   170→export function $isImageNode(
   171→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
   172→): node is ImageNode {
   173→  return node instanceof ImageNode
   174→}
   175→
```
</details></tool-use>

    39→          ])

    66→  }

    70→  }

 `./packages/rich-editor/src/components/RendererWrapper.tsx`

```text
     1→import type { ComponentType, ReactElement } from 'react'
     2→import { createElement } from 'react'
     3→
     4→import { useRendererConfig } from '../context/RendererConfigContext'
     5→import type { RendererConfig } from '../types/renderer-config'
     6→
     7→export type RendererKey = keyof RendererConfig
     8→
     9→type RendererPropsByKey = {
    10→  [K in RendererKey]-?: NonNullable<RendererConfig[K]> extends ComponentType<
    11→    infer P extends object
    12→  >
    13→    ? P
    14→    : never
    15→}
    16→
    17→type RendererComponentByKey = {
    18→  [K in RendererKey]-?: NonNullable<
    19→    RendererConfig[K]
    20→  > extends ComponentType<any>
    21→    ? NonNullable<RendererConfig[K]>
    22→    : never
    23→}
    24→
    25→export type RendererWrapperProps = {
    26→  [K in RendererKey]-?: {
    27→    /** Name of the renderer in RendererConfig */
    28→    rendererKey: K
    29→    /** Default renderer component */
    30→    defaultRenderer: RendererComponentByKey[K]
    31→    /** Props to pass to the renderer */
    32→    props: RendererPropsByKey[K]
    33→  }
    34→}[RendererKey]
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Wrapper component that allows overriding default renderers with custom ones.
    38→ * Uses RendererConfig from context to determine which renderer to use.
    39→ */
    40→export function RendererWrapper({
    41→  rendererKey,
    42→  defaultRenderer: DefaultRenderer,
    43→  props,
    44→}: RendererWrapperProps) {
    45→  const config = useRendererConfig()
    46→  const Renderer: ComponentType<any> = config?.[rendererKey] ?? DefaultRenderer
    47→
    48→  return <Renderer {...props} />
    49→}
    50→
    51→/**
    52→ * Type-safe helper for creating RendererWrapper elements from .ts node files.
    53→ * Avoids the createElement + discriminated-union typing limitation.
    54→ */
    55→export function createRendererDecoration<K extends RendererKey>(
    56→  rendererKey: K,
    57→  defaultRenderer: RendererComponentByKey[K],
    58→  props: RendererPropsByKey[K],
    59→): ReactElement {
    60→  return createElement(RendererWrapper, {
    61→    rendererKey,
    62→    defaultRenderer,
    63→    props,
    64→  } as RendererWrapperProps)
    65→}
    66→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-25 15:19:55Z)**_

Perfect. I have all the information about the Lexical node type to CSS class name mappings. Let me create a comprehensive reference document.

## Lexical Node Type → CSS Class Name Mapping Reference

Based on the Shiroi codebase, here is the **exact class name mapping** that Lexical applies to each node type:

### Text Format Classes
```
theme.text.bold              → 'rich-text-bold'
theme.text.italic            → 'rich-text-italic'
theme.text.underline         → 'rich-text-underline'
theme.text.strikethrough     → 'rich-text-strikethrough'
theme.text.superscript       → 'rich-text-superscript'
theme.text.subscript         → 'rich-text-subscript'
theme.text.code              → 'rich-text-code'
theme.text.highlight         → 'rich-text-highlight'
```

### Block Structure Classes

**Paragraph:**
```
theme.paragraph              → 'rich-paragraph'
```

**Headings (h1–h6):**
```
theme.heading.h1             → 'rich-heading-h1'
theme.heading.h2             → 'rich-heading-h2'
theme.heading.h3             → 'rich-heading-h3'
theme.heading.h4             → 'rich-heading-h4'
theme.heading.h5             → 'rich-heading-h5'
theme.heading.h6             → 'rich-heading-h6'
```

**Lists:**
```
theme.list.ol                → 'rich-list-ol'           (ordered list)
theme.list.ul                → 'rich-list-ul'           (unordered list)
theme.list.listitem          → 'rich-list-item'         (regular list item)
theme.list.nested.listitem   → 'rich-list-nested-item'  (nested list item, listStyleType: none)
theme.list.checklist         → 'rich-checklist'         (checklist container)
theme.list.listitemChecked   → 'rich-list-item-checked' (checked checkbox item)
theme.list.listitemUnchecked → 'rich-list-item-unchecked'
```

**Blockquote:**
```
theme.quote                  → 'rich-quote'
```

**Link:**
```
theme.link                   → 'rich-link'
```

**Horizontal Rule:**
```
theme.hr                     → 'rich-hr'
```

**Code Block:**
```
theme.code                   → 'rich-code-block'
```

### Table Classes

```
theme.table                  → 'rich-table'
theme.tableCell              → 'rich-table-cell'        (regular td)
theme.tableCellHeader        → 'rich-table-cell-header'  (th with uppercase text, sticky top)
theme.tableScrollableWrapper → 'rich-table-scrollable-wrapper'
```

### Special Node Classes (from shared.css.ts)

```
'rich-spoiler'                           (spoiler text element)
'rich-spoiler-revealed'                  (spoiler after click)
'rich-footnote'                          (footnote reference marker)
'rich-footnote-ref'                      (footnote badge link)
'rich-footnote-ref-wrapper'              (inline wrapper)
'rich-footnote-section'                  (footnote section container)
'rich-footnote-section-divider'          (hr between content and footnotes)
'rich-footnote-section-list'             (decimal list of footnotes)
'rich-footnote-section-item'             (li item in footnote list)
'rich-footnote-section-item-edit'        (edit mode: flex container for #, input, remove btn)
'rich-footnote-section-item-num'         (footnote number label in edit)
'rich-footnote-section-item-input'       (edit input for footnote content)
'rich-footnote-section-item-remove'      (delete button for footnote in edit)
'rich-footnote-back-ref'                 (back arrow link from footnote to content)
'rich-footnote-highlight'                (animation flash on footnote click)
```

### Heading Anchor Classes
```
'rich-heading-anchor'        (# link icon on h1-h6, positioned absolutely left)
```

### Provider Chain & Configuration (RichRenderer.tsx)

The `RichRenderer` uses:
1. **`LexicalComposer` initialConfig:**
   - `theme: editorTheme` (from `theme.ts`) — applies all class names above
   - `nodes: allNodes` — static node classes
   - `editable: false` — read-only mode
   - `editorState: JSON.stringify(value)` — serialized Lexical state

2. **Provider Stack (for nested/context):**
   - `PortalThemeProvider` — wraps theme variants
   - `ColorSchemeContext` — light/dark variant
   - `RendererConfigProvider` — custom renderer injection
   - `NestedContentRendererProvider` — nested content support

3. **Plugins:**
   - `FootnotePlugin` — adds footnote section rendering
   - `HeadingAnchorPlugin` — adds `.rich-heading-anchor` links to h1-h6

4. **Content Structure:**
   ```jsx
   <Component className="rich-content {variantClass}" data-theme={theme}>
     <LexicalComposer>
       <FootnotePlugin>
         <RichTextPlugin>
           <ContentEditable className="rich-content__body" />
         </RichTextPlugin>
         <HeadingAnchorPlugin />
       </FootnotePlugin>
     </LexicalComposer>
   </Component>
   ```

### CSS Variable System (vars.css.ts)

All styles use Vanilla Extract theme contracts. The `richContent` container class applies:
- `fontFamily: vars.typography.fontFamily`
- `fontSize: vars.typography.fontSizeBase`
- `lineHeight: vars.typography.lineHeight`
- `color: vars.color.text`
- All child styles are scoped with `${richContent} .class-name` selectors

### Key Styling Details for SSR Renderer

**Nested List Styling:**
- `.rich-list-ol` within `.rich-list-nested-item` → `listStyleType: 'lower-alpha'`
- `.rich-list-ul` within `.rich-list-nested-item` → `listStyleType: 'circle'`

**Checklist Items:**
- `.rich-list-item-checked` has `::before` checkbox (filled) and `::after` checkmark
- `.rich-list-item-unchecked` has `::before` checkbox (empty)
- Both apply `paddingLeft: 1.9em` to make room for checkbox

**Table Headers:**
- `.rich-table-cell-header` has `position: sticky; top: 0;` for header row
- Uppercase text with `fontSize: 0.75em`, `textTransform: 'uppercase'`

**Paragraph Spacing:**
- Base margin: `marginBottom: 1em`
- First/last child of `.rich-content` have `marginTop: 0` / `marginBottom: 0`

This is the **complete mapping** needed for your SSR renderer to produce identical HTML/classes to the LexicalComposer-based renderer.

    17→  const selected = presets.find((p) => p.key === selectedKey) || presets[0]

    30→                  selectedKey === preset.key
    31→                    ? 'preset-item preset-item-active'
    32→                    : 'preset-item'
    33→                }
    34→                onClick={() => setSelectedKey(preset.key)}
    35→              >
    36→                <div className="preset-item-label">{preset.label}</div>
    37→                <div className="preset-item-desc">{preset.description}</div>
    38→              </button>
    39→            ))}
    40→          </div>
    41→        </aside>
    42→
    43→        {/* Main content */}
    44→        <div className="presets-main">
    45→          {/* Toolbar */}
    46→          <div className="toolbar">
    47→            <div className="toolbar-group">
    48→              <span className="toolbar-label">Mode</span>
    49→              <button
    50→                className={mode === 'edit' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    51→                onClick={() => setMode('edit')}
    52→              >
    53→                Edit
    54→              </button>
    55→              <button
    56→                className={mode === 'readonly' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    57→                onClick={() => setMode('readonly')}
    58→              >
    59→                Readonly
    60→              </button>
    61→              <button
    62→                className={mode === 'ssr' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    63→                onClick={() => setMode('ssr')}
    64→              >
    65→                SSR
    66→              </button>
    67→            </div>
    68→            <div className="toolbar-group">
    69→              <span className="toolbar-label">Variant</span>
    70→              <button
    71→                className={variant === 'article' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    72→                onClick={() => setVariant('article')}
    73→              >
    74→                Article
    75→              </button>
    76→              <button
    77→                className={variant === 'comment' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    78→                onClick={() => setVariant('comment')}
    79→              >
    80→                Comment
    81→              </button>
    82→              <button
    83→                className={variant === 'note' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
    84→                onClick={() => setVariant('note')}
    85→              >
    86→                Note
    87→              </button>
    88→            </div>
    89→          </div>
    90→
    91→          {/* Renderer */}
    92→          <Panel title={selected.label} badge={`${variant} · ${mode}`}>
    93→            {mode === 'edit' ? (
    94→              <ShiroEditor
    95→                key={`${selected.key}-edit`}
    96→                initialValue={selected.data}
    97→                variant={variant}
    98→                theme={theme}
    99→              />
   100→            ) : mode === 'readonly' ? (
   101→              <ShiroRenderer
   102→                key={`${selected.key}-readonly`}
   103→                value={selected.data}
   104→                variant={variant}
   105→                theme={theme}
   106→              />
   107→            ) : (
   108→              <ShiroSSRRenderer
   109→                key={`${selected.key}-ssr`}
   110→                value={selected.data}
   111→                variant={variant}
   112→                theme={theme}
   113→              />
   114→            )}
   115→          </Panel>
   116→
   117→          {/* JSON */}
   118→          <Panel title="Serialized JSON">
   119→            <JsonViewer data={selected.data} defaultExpanded={false} />
   120→          </Panel>
   121→        </div>
   122→      </div>
   123→    </div>
   124→  )
   125→}
   126→
```
</details></tool-use>

Now let me check if there's a `renderBuiltinNode` function to see how it handles decorator nodes:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-25 15:20:04Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-ssr-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx`

```text
     1→import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
     2→
     3→function textToSlug(text: string): string {
     4→  return text
     5→    .toLowerCase()
     6→    .trim()
     7→    .replaceAll(/[^\w\s\u3000-\u9fff\uac00-\ud7af\uff00-\uffef-]/g, '')
     8→    .replaceAll(/[\s_]+/g, '-')
     9→    .replaceAll(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    10→}
    11→
    12→function extractText(node: any): string {
    13→  if (node.text) return node.text
    14→  if (node.children) return node.children.map(extractText).join('')
    15→  return ''
    16→}
    17→
    18→export function renderBuiltinNode(
    19→  node: any,
    20→  key: string,
    21→  children: ReactNode[] | null,
    22→  headingSlugs: Map<string, number>,
    23→): ReactNode {
    24→  switch (node.type) {
    25→    case 'root':
    26→      return <>{children}</>
    27→    case 'paragraph': {
    28→      const align = node.format ? { textAlign: node.format } as const : undefined
    29→      return <p key={key} className="rich-paragraph" style={align}>{children}</p>
    30→    }
    31→    case 'heading': {
    32→      const Tag = node.tag as 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6'
    33→      const text = extractText(node)
    34→      const baseSlug = textToSlug(text)
    35→      let slug = baseSlug
    36→      if (baseSlug) {
    37→        const count = headingSlugs.get(baseSlug)
    38→        if (count !== undefined) {
    39→          slug = `${baseSlug}-${count}`
    40→          headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, count + 1)
    41→        } else {
    42→          headingSlugs.set(baseSlug, 1)
    43→        }
    44→      }
    45→      return (
    46→        <Tag key={key} id={slug || undefined} className={`rich-heading-${Tag}`}>
    47→          {slug && <a className="rich-heading-anchor" aria-hidden="true" tabIndex={-1} href={`#${slug}`} />}
    48→          {children}
    49→        </Tag>
    50→      )
    51→    }
    52→    case 'quote':
    53→      return <blockquote key={key} className="rich-quote">{children}</blockquote>
    54→    case 'list': {
    55→      const Tag = node.listType === 'number' ? 'ol' : 'ul'
    56→      const cls = node.listType === 'number' ? 'rich-list-ol'
    57→        : node.listType === 'check' ? 'rich-checklist rich-list-ul' : 'rich-list-ul'
    58→      return <Tag key={key} className={cls} start={node.start !== 1 ? node.start : undefined}>{children}</Tag>
    59→    }
    60→    case 'listitem': {
    61→      const isChecklist = node.checked !== undefined
    62→      const cls = isChecklist
    63→        ? (node.checked ? 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-checked' : 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-unchecked')
    64→        : 'rich-list-item'
    65→      return <li key={key} className={cls} value={node.value}>{children}</li>
    66→    }
    67→    case 'link':
    68→      return <a key={key} className="rich-link" href={node.url} target={node.target} rel={node.rel}>{children}</a>
    69→    case 'autolink':
    70→      return <a key={key} className="rich-link" href={node.url} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">{children}</a>
    71→    case 'horizontalrule':
    72→      return <hr key={key} className="rich-hr" />
    73→    case 'table':
    74→      return (
    75→        <div key={key} className="rich-table-scrollable-wrapper">
    76→          <table className="rich-table">{children}</table>
    77→        </div>
    78→      )
    79→    case 'tablerow':
    80→      return <tr key={key}>{children}</tr>
    81→    case 'tablecell': {
    82→      const CellTag = node.headerState ? 'th' : 'td'
    83→      const cls = node.headerState ? 'rich-table-cell-header' : 'rich-table-cell'
    84→      return <CellTag key={key} className={cls} colSpan={node.colSpan > 1 ? node.colSpan : undefined}>{children}</CellTag>
    85→    }
    86→    case 'code':
    87→      return <pre key={key} className="rich-code-block"><code>{children}</code></pre>
    88→    case 'code-highlight':
    89→      return <span key={key}>{node.text}</span>
    90→    case 'linebreak':
    91→      return <br key={key} />
    92→    case 'tab':
    93→      return <span key={key}>{'  '}</span>
    94→    default:
    95→      return null
    96→  }
    97→}
    98→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import type { SerializedEditorState } from 'lexical'
     2→import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
     3→import { createContext, use } from 'react'
     4→
     5→export type RenderEditorStateFn = (state: SerializedEditorState) => ReactNode
     6→
     7→const NestedContentRendererContext = createContext<RenderEditorStateFn | null>(
     8→  null,
     9→)
    10→
    11→export const NestedContentRendererProvider =
    12→  NestedContentRendererContext.Provider
    13→
    14→export function useNestedContentRenderer(): RenderEditorStateFn {
    15→  const fn = use(NestedContentRendererContext)
    16→  if (!fn) {
    17→    throw new Error(
    18→      'useNestedContentRenderer must be used within a NestedContentRendererProvider',
    19→    )
    20→  }
    21→  return fn
    22→}
    23→
```
</details></tool-use>

  category: 'inline' | 'block' | 'container'
  data: SerializedEditorState
}

export const nodeSamples: NodeSample[] = [
  // Inline Nodes
--
      alertQuote(
        'note',
        paragraph(
          text('This is a note alert. Use it for additional information.'),
        ),
      ) as any,
    ),
  },
  {
    key: 'alert-tip',
    label: 'Alert - Tip',
    description: 'Tip alert type',
    category: 'container',
    data: doc(
      alertQuote(
        'tip',
        paragraph(text('💡 Pro tip: Always test your code before deploying!')),
      ) as any,
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-25 15:20:57Z)**_

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me synthesize my findings into a comprehensive analysis document.

## Root Cause Analysis: DecoratorNodes Not Rendering in SSR

Based on my investigation of the codebase, I've identified the core issue preventing DecoratorNodes (AlertQuoteNode, BannerNode, GridContainerNode, etc.) from rendering in SSR mode.

### Critical Finding: `getDecorators()` Returns Empty in Headless Mode

The fundamental problem is in **RichSSRRenderer.tsx (line 69)**:

```typescript
const decorations = editor.getDecorators<ReactNode>()
```

In Lexical's headless editor implementation, `getDecorators()` **does not automatically invoke the `decorate()` methods** of DecoratorNodes. The decorators map is only populated when:
1. The editor is in edit mode (interactive)
2. Decorators are explicitly registered or rendered through the interactive render cycle

In headless/SSR mode, `getDecorators()` returns an **empty object** `{}`, which is why the decorator lookup at **line 29-31** fails:

```typescript
if (decorations[nodeKey] !== undefined) {
  return decorations[nodeKey]
}
```

### Data Flow

Here's what happens in each render mode:

**Edit Mode (RichEditor)**
- Lexical updates → triggers node `decorate()` methods
- `decorate()` returns `ReactElement` 
- Editor stores result in decorators map
- `getDecorators()` returns populated map

**SSR Mode (RichSSRRenderer)**
- Creates headless editor
- Sets editor state
- Calls `getDecorators()` → **returns `{}`** (empty, never populated)
- `renderTree()` checks decorations lookup → always undefined
- Falls through to `renderBuiltinNode()` which has no handlers for decorator node types
- Result: **`null`** is returned (default case in switch statement)

### Evidence from Code Structure

**Pattern 1: All DecoratorNodes use `decorate()` to return ReactElements**

- `AlertQuoteNode.decorate()` → `createElement(AlertStaticDecorator, ...)`
- `BannerNode.decorate()` → `createElement(BannerStaticDecorator, ...)`
- `GridContainerNode.decorate()` → `createElement(GridStaticDecorator, ...)`
- `ImageNode.decorate()` → `createRendererDecoration('Image', ImageRenderer, ...)`
- `MermaidNode.decorate()` → `createRendererDecoration('Mermaid', MermaidRenderer, ...)`
- `VideoNode.decorate()` → `createRendererDecoration('Video', VideoRenderer, ...)`

All extend `DecoratorNode<ReactElement>` and use the `decorate()` method.

**Pattern 2: `renderBuiltinNode` doesn't handle DecoratorNode types**

Looking at `packages/rich-ssr-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx`, the switch statement (line 24) only handles:
- `'root'`, `'paragraph'`, `'heading'`, `'quote'`, `'list'`, `'listitem'`, `'link'`, `'autolink'`, `'horizontalrule'`, `'table'`, `'tablerow'`, `'tablecell'`, `'code'`, `'code-highlight'`, `'linebreak'`, `'tab'`

It does **NOT** handle:
- `'alert-quote'`
- `'banner'`
- `'grid-container'`
- `'image'`
- `'mermaid'`
- `'video'`
- `'link-card'`
- `'katex-block'`
- `'katex-inline'`
- Any other DecoratorNode type

So when a DecoratorNode's type is passed in, it hits the `default: return null` (line 94-95).

**Pattern 3: Nested content rendering uses a provider function**

The `AlertStaticDecorator` component uses `useNestedContentRenderer()` to render nested `SerializedEditorState`. This context is set up in `RichSSRRenderer` (line 81-103) with `renderNestedContent` function that recursively calls `renderEditorToReact()`.

But this function **is only available inside React components** that are rendered. If DecoratorNodes never return their decorator components, this nested rendering never happens.

### Why This Wasn't Caught Earlier

1. **Edit Mode Works Fine**: In interactive mode, Lexical's render system invokes `decorate()` automatically
2. **Decorator Nodes Are in Config**: They're registered in `allNodes` so deserialization works
3. **No Errors Thrown**: Returning `null` from `renderBuiltinNode` silently disappears nodes instead of erroring
4. **Different Code Paths**: Edit mode uses Lexical's renderer; SSR uses custom headless renderer

### The Fix Requires

Two complementary approaches:

**Approach A: Manually invoke `decorate()` for DecoratorNodes in renderTree**
- When a node type is not handled by `renderBuiltinNode`, check if it's a DecoratorNode
- Manually call its `decorate()` method to get the ReactElement
- Return that element directly

**Approach B: Handle all DecoratorNode types in `renderBuiltinNode`**
- For each DecoratorNode type, extract data from the serialized node
- Render the appropriate static decorator component directly
- Pass nested content state to context for rendering

**Key Consideration**: The `decorate()` method takes `(editor: LexicalEditor, config: EditorConfig)` parameters. In headless mode, you have the editor instance, but these are typically not used in static decorators. However, some nodes like `MermaidNode.decorate()` reference `this.__key` to register callbacks — these won't work in SSR without modification.

### Demo Verification

In `PresetsPage.tsx` (line 108-113), there's an SSR mode toggle:
```typescript
} else (
  <ShiroSSRRenderer
    key={`${selected.key}-ssr`}
    value={selected.data}
    variant={variant}
    theme={theme}
  />
)
```

When you switch to SSR mode with an alert-quote, banner, or grid fixture, these nodes will not render (appearing as missing content) because of this bug.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 15:21:50Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a clear understanding of the problem:

1. **Root Cause**: `editor.getDecorators()` in headless mode returns an EMPTY object because the headless editor doesn't automatically invoke `decorate()` methods. The decorators are only populated in the interactive render cycle.

2. **Built-in Node Classes**: The `renderBuiltinNode` function uses the correct class names (they match the `editorTheme` mapping: `rich-paragraph`, `rich-heading-h1`, etc.) — so the classes ARE correct. But the SSR renderer is missing some features like:
   - Heading anchor links (from `HeadingAnchorPlugin`)
   - Footnote section (from `FootnotePlugin`)
   - Nested list item styling (`rich-list-nested-item`)

3. **DecoratorNode rendering**: All DecoratorNodes (Alert, Banner, Grid, Image, Mermaid, Video, etc.) are completely missing because `getDecorators()` returns empty.

The fix is to manually invoke `decorate()` on each DecoratorNode during tree traversal so we can capture the React elements they produce, rather than relying on the empty decorators object.

I need to check what parameters `decorate()` actually expects—the editor instance is available from the outer scope, but I'm not sure if accessing `editor._config` directly is the right approach or if there's a public API for getting the EditorConfig.

Looking at how decorator nodes typically work, most implementations don't actually need those parameters since they can access their own key and stored data through `this`. I could simplify this by checking if a node has a `decorate()` method during traversal and calling it with the editor and config, wrapping it in a try-catch to handle any errors gracefully.

The decorators return React elements that get rendered with full access to the context providers. Now I'm checking whether the built-in node renderers already align with the theme class names, and it looks like they do match up correctly.

The old RichRenderer had a few extra features like heading anchors and footnote processing, but those are already handled in the current implementation. The real gap is just that decorator nodes need to explicitly call `decorate()` to render their content properly.

Now I'm looking at how nested list items are handled — the current code assigns a single `rich-list-item` class to all list items, but Lexical's theme distinguishes between regular and nested list items with separate classes. A nested list item is one that contains another list inside it, and it should get the `rich-list-nested-item` class instead.

For the checklist rendering, the class order is `'rich-checklist rich-list-ul'`, but Lexical applies the checklist class separately on the `<ul>` element itself.

Now I'm comparing the wrapper structure between the old RichRenderer and the SSR renderer. The old version nests content inside a `rich-content__body` div within the LexicalComposer, while the SSR version renders directly without that intermediate wrapper. This structural difference could affect styling or layout behavior.

Looking at the CSS in `shared.css.ts`, the styles target the `richContent` container and its child selectors like `${richContent} .rich-paragraph`, so they're not specifically dependent on the `rich-content__body` wrapper from Lexical's ContentEditable component. The styling should work the same way in both approaches.

So the focus should be on fixing the actual rendering differences rather than worrying about the wrapper structure.

Now I'm thinking through the text formatting logic. The text node rendering applies format flags using a bitmask system where bold, italic, strikethrough, and underline are encoded as individual bits...

Checking the class names against the theme to make sure they all match up correctly.

Now I need to verify the implementation in `renderTextNode.tsx` and update the plan accordingly. The key thing I'm thinking through is whether calling `node.decorate(editor, config)` during `editorState.read()` will work—it should since we're inside the read callback with a live node instance that can return a ReactElement, though I'm slightly concerned about whether some nodes might try to check `editor.isEditable()` in their decorate methods.

Looking at the specific nodes, the refactored ones don't do this check, and for unreformed nodes like Image and Video, their `decorate()` methods use `createRendererDecoration()` which wraps in `RendererWrapper` without checking editability. For TldrawNode and GalleryNode after Phase 1 changes, they'll return the appropriate static renderers without edit callbacks, so the approach should work cleanly—I just need to replace the `getDecorators()` call with the direct `node.decorate()` invocation.

Updating the plan now...
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-ssr-renderer/src/engine/renderTextNode.tsx`

```text
     1→import type { ReactNode } from 'react'
     2→
     3→const FORMAT_FLAGS: [number, string][] = [
     4→  [1, 'rich-text-bold'],
     5→  [2, 'rich-text-italic'],
     6→  [4, 'rich-text-strikethrough'],
     7→  [8, 'rich-text-underline'],
     8→  [16, 'rich-text-code'],
     9→  [32, 'rich-text-subscript'],
    10→  [64, 'rich-text-superscript'],
    11→  [128, 'rich-text-highlight'],
    12→]
    13→
    14→export function renderTextNode(node: any, key: string): ReactNode {
    15→  let element: ReactNode = node.text
    16→  const format = node.format || 0
    17→
    18→  for (const [flag, className] of FORMAT_FLAGS) {
    19→    if (format & flag) {
    20→      element = <span key={`${key}-${flag}`} className={className}>{element}</span>
    21→    }
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  return element
    25→}
    26→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 15:22:04Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Good. The text format classes match the `editorTheme`. Now let me also verify one more thing about the `renderBuiltinNode` - the `code-highlight` case. In Lexical, code-highlight tokens have specific highlight type classes. Let me check if the theme has these...Actually, in the editorTheme (from theme.ts), there's `theme.codeHighlight` which maps highlight types to classes. The current `renderBuiltinNode` just renders code-highlight as `<span>{node.text}</span>` without any highlight class. This means code blocks in SSR won't have syntax highlighting colors. But that might be acceptable since syntax highlighting is typically done by Shiki in the CodeBlockRenderer.


Actually, CodeBlockNode is a DecoratorNode, not a built-in, so the `code` case in `renderBuiltinNode` only handles Lexical's built-in CodeNode. The custom CodeBlockNode goes through the decorator path instead, so that's fine.

Now I have what I need. The core fix is replacing `editor.getDecorators()` with inline `node.decorate(editor, config)` calls during tree traversal, and I need to add a couple of additional fixes in `renderBuiltinNode`.

Updating the plan file with the nested list item styling and paragraph alignment verification...

```md
# 修复 SSR 渲染器 + 统一为唯一 RichRenderer

## Context

SSR 渲染器 (`RichSSRRenderer`) 未能复刻 Readonly Renderer 的效果。从截图对比可见：
- **Alert 块完全消失**（SSR 下无 TIP 容器，仅剩后续文字）
- **Tldraw 只剩一条细线**（SSR placeholder 过于简陋）

**根因**：`editor.getDecorators()` 在 `@lexical/headless` 模式下返回空对象 `{}`。Lexical 的 decorator 机制仅在交互式渲染周期中填充，headless editor 不触发 `decorate()` 调用。导致所有 DecoratorNode（Alert、Banner、Grid、Image、Mermaid、Video、LinkCard、KaTeX、Tldraw、Gallery、CodeBlock 等）全部丢失。

修复后再执行包改名与统一。

---

## Part A：修复 SSR 引擎渲染

### A1：修复 DecoratorNode 渲染

**文件**：`packages/rich-ssr-renderer/src/RichSSRRenderer.tsx`

**问题**：`editor.getDecorators<ReactNode>()` 返回 `{}`，`renderTree` 中 `decorations[nodeKey]` 永远 `undefined`。

**修复**：在 `renderTree` 遍历中直接调用 `node.decorate(editor, config)` 代替 `getDecorators()` 查表。

\```tsx
function renderTree(
  node: any,
  editor: LexicalEditor,
  headingSlugs: Map<string, number>,
  key: string,
): ReactNode {
  const nodeKey = node.getKey ? node.getKey() : key

  // DecoratorNode: 直接调用 decorate() 获取 ReactElement
  if (typeof node.decorate === 'function') {
    try {
      return node.decorate(editor, editor._config)
    } catch {
      return null
    }
  }

  const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}

  if (serialized.type === 'text') {
    return renderTextNode(serialized, nodeKey)
  }

  let children: ReactNode[] | null = null
  if (node.getChildren) {
    const childNodes = node.getChildren()
    if (childNodes.length > 0) {
      children = childNodes.map((child: any, i: number) =>
        renderTree(child, editor, headingSlugs, `${nodeKey}-${i}`),
      )
    }
  }

  return renderBuiltinNode(serialized, nodeKey, children, headingSlugs)
}
\```

**关键变更**：
- 移除 `decorations` 参数，移除 `editor.getDecorators()` 调用
- 新增 `editor` 参数传递到 `renderTree`
- DecoratorNode 检测：`typeof node.decorate === 'function'`
- 直接 `node.decorate(editor, editor._config)` 返回 ReactElement
- `renderEditorToReact` 和 `renderNestedContent` 同步更新签名

**安全性**：
- `editable: false` 确保所有 static node 的 `decorate()` 走静态路径
- 无 `isEditable()` 分支（Phase 1 已消除）
- 返回的 ReactElement 在 Provider 链内渲染，context hooks 正常工作

### A2：补全 `renderBuiltinNode` 缺失特性

**文件**：`packages/rich-ssr-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx`

已确认 class names 与 `editorTheme` 一致（`rich-paragraph`、`rich-heading-h1`、`rich-quote`、`rich-link`、`rich-hr` 等）。

需补全：
1. **nested listitem**：当 `<li>` 包含子 list 时，添加 `rich-list-nested-item` class
2. **details/spoiler**：若 `DetailsNode` 等非 Decorator 的 ElementNode 需手动处理（检查 DetailsNode 是否 DecoratorNode — 是的话已由 A1 覆盖）

\```tsx
case 'listitem': {
  const isChecklist = node.checked !== undefined
  // 检查是否嵌套（children 中含 list）
  const hasNestedList = children && children.some(/* check */)
  const isNested = node.nested ?? false  // Lexical serializes this
  let cls = 'rich-list-item'
  if (isChecklist) {
    cls = node.checked ? 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-checked' : 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-unchecked'
  }
  if (isNested) {
    cls = 'rich-list-nested-item'
  }
  return <li key={key} className={cls} value={node.value}>{children}</li>
}
\```

注：需确认 Lexical 序列化格式中嵌套 listitem 的标识方式（可能是 children 中包含 list 节点）。

### A3：验证 text format 渲染

**文件**：`packages/rich-ssr-renderer/src/engine/renderTextNode.tsx`

已确认 class names 匹配 `editorTheme.text.*`：
- `rich-text-bold`、`rich-text-italic`、`rich-text-strikethrough`、`rich-text-underline`
- `rich-text-code`、`rich-text-subscript`、`rich-text-superscript`、`rich-text-highlight`

无需修改。

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-25 15:33:50Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user provided excellent feedback from a code review. Let me address all the findings:1. **Finding 1** (CRITICAL) - Confirmed. All DecoratorNodes are affected. Already addressed in plan.

2. **Finding 2** (CRITICAL) - `HorizontalRuleNode` is a DecoratorNode (from `@lexical/extension`) that doesn't override `decorate()`. The default `decorate()` returns `null`. If we just check `typeof node.decorate === 'function'` and return the result, `null` would cause the `<hr>` to disappear. **Fix**: If `decorate()` returns `null`/`undefined`, fall through to the builtin rendering.


3. **Finding 3** (HIGH) - `DetailsNode` and `SpoilerNode` are custom ElementNodes with children, but `renderBuiltinNode` doesn't handle them, so they fall through to the default case and disappear. I need to add cases for both `details` and `spoiler` nodes.

4. **Finding 4** (MEDIUM) - The demo pages also pull from the same SSR module, so I'll need to update those imports as well.

5. **Finding 5** - Still reviewing the remaining findings.

Now I'm checking whether `@haklex/rich-ext-embed` actually exports a style.css file, since the plan suggested it might not exist. For the open questions, the fallback strategy for DecoratorNodes makes sense—try `decorate()` first, and if it returns nothing, use the builtin rendering. That handles HorizontalRuleNode cleanly. Since this is a closed-source project, I can skip backward compatibility aliases and just make the breaking changes. Let me consolidate these fixes into the plan and move forward.
</details></think>

    27→export class DetailsNode extends ElementNode {

    45→
    46→  static getType(): string {
    47→    return 'details'

    52→  }
    53→
    54→  constructor(summary: string, open = false, key?: NodeKey) {
    55→    super(key)
    56→    this.__summary = summary
    57→    this.__open = open
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    61→    const details = document.createElement('details')
    62→    details.className = 'rich-details'
    63→    if (this.__open) {
    64→      details.open = true
    65→    }
    66→
    67→    const summary = document.createElement('summary')
    68→    summary.className = 'rich-details-summary'
    69→
    70→    const label = document.createElement('span')
    71→    label.className = 'rich-details-summary-text'
    72→    label.textContent = this.__summary
    73→    summary.append(label)
    74→

    77→    chevron.append(

    91→    return details
    92→  }
    93→
    94→  updateDOM(prevNode: DetailsNode, dom: HTMLElement): boolean {
    95→    const details = dom as HTMLDetailsElement
    96→    if (prevNode.__open !== this.__open) {
    97→      details.open = this.__open

    99→    if (prevNode.__summary !== this.__summary) {
   100→      const label = dom.querySelector('.rich-details-summary-text')
   101→      if (label) {

   110→  }
   111→
   112→  exportJSON(): SerializedDetailsNode {

   115→      type: 'details',

   117→      open: this.__open,
   118→      version: 1,
   119→    }
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  getSummary(): string {
   123→    return this.getLatest().__summary
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  setSummary(summary: string): void {
   127→    const writable = this.getWritable()

   129→  }
   130→

   133→  }
   134→

   137→    writable.__open = open
   138→  }
   139→

- BannerNode → `banner` (DecoratorNode, decorate() → BannerStaticDecorator)
- GridContainerNode → `grid-container` (DecoratorNode, decorate() → GridStaticDecorator)
- ImageNode → `image` (DecoratorNode, decorate() → ImageStaticDecorator)
- VideoNode → `video` (DecoratorNode, decorate() → RendererWrapper)
- MermaidNode → `mermaid` (DecoratorNode, decorate() → RendererWrapper)

- FootnoteNode → `footnote` (DecoratorNode, decorate() → RendererWrapper)
- FootnoteSectionNode → `footnote-section` (DecoratorNode, decorate() → RendererWrapper)

I'm also noticing that SpoilerNode and DetailsNode are custom ElementNodes that aren't DecoratorNodes and aren't handled in renderBuiltinNode, which means they're missing implementations. There's also a special case with HorizontalRuleNode from the Lexical extension—its decorate() returns null, so I need to figure out how the A1 fix handles that scenario.

The plan is shaping up: first, I need to update renderTree to check if decorate() returns a truthy ReactElement and fall through to builtin rendering if it doesn't. Then add spoiler and details cases to renderBuiltinNode, update the embed styles in the CSS file, and make sure both EditorPage and PresetsPage get the same updates as DiffPage. <tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# 修复 SSR 渲染器 + 统一为唯一 RichRenderer

## Context

SSR 渲染器未复刻 Readonly Renderer 效果。根因：`editor.getDecorators()` 在 headless 模式下返回空 `{}`，所有 DecoratorNode 丢失。此外 `DetailsNode`/`SpoilerNode`（ElementNode）无 builtin case 亦消失。

---

## Part A：修复 SSR 引擎渲染

### A1：修复 DecoratorNode 渲染

**文件**：`packages/rich-ssr-renderer/src/RichSSRRenderer.tsx`

**根因**：`editor.getDecorators()` 在 headless 下返回 `{}`。

**修复**：移除 `getDecorators()` 调用，在 `renderTree` 中对 DecoratorNode 直接调用 `node.decorate(editor, editor._config)`。

**关键**：`decorate()` 返回 `null`/`undefined` 时（如 `HorizontalRuleNode`），继续 fallthrough 到 builtin 分支。

\```tsx
function renderTree(
  node: any,
  editor: LexicalEditor,
  headingSlugs: Map<string, number>,
  key: string,
): ReactNode {
  const nodeKey = node.getKey ? node.getKey() : key

  // DecoratorNode: 调用 decorate()，返回 null 则 fallthrough
  if (typeof node.decorate === 'function') {
    try {
      const decoration = node.decorate(editor, editor._config)
      if (decoration != null) return decoration
    } catch { /* fallthrough to builtin */ }
  }

  const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}

  if (serialized.type === 'text') {
    return renderTextNode(serialized, nodeKey)
  }

  let children: ReactNode[] | null = null
  if (node.getChildren) {
    const childNodes = node.getChildren()
    if (childNodes.length > 0) {
      children = childNodes.map((child: any, i: number) =>
        renderTree(child, editor, headingSlugs, `${nodeKey}-${i}`),
      )
    }
  }

  return renderBuiltinNode(serialized, nodeKey, children, headingSlugs)
}
\```

**变更清单**：
- 移除 `decorations` 参数和 `editor.getDecorators()` 调用
- `renderTree` 新增 `editor` 参数（替代 `decorations`）
- DecoratorNode 分支：`decorate()` 返回非 null → use，否则 fallthrough
- `renderEditorToReact` 内传 `editor` 给 `renderTree`
- `renderNestedContent` 同步更新

**影响节点**（全部 DecoratorNode 均通过此路径渲染）：
- Alert、Banner、Grid → 各自的 StaticDecorator
- Image、Video、Mermaid、CodeBlock、LinkCard、KaTeX、Mention、Footnote、FootnoteSection → RendererWrapper
- HorizontalRuleNode → `decorate()` 返回 null → fallthrough → `renderBuiltinNode` 的 `<hr>` 分支
- Tldraw、Gallery、Embed、CodeSnippet → 各自的 static decorate

### A2：补全 ElementNode — details + spoiler

**文件**：`packages/rich-ssr-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx`

这两个是自定义 ElementNode（非 DecoratorNode），当前 switch 无 case，命中 `default: null`。

**新增 case**：

\```tsx
case 'details': {
  // DetailsNode: <details class="rich-details"> + <summary> + <div class="rich-details-content">children</div>
  const summary = node.summary || ''
  return (
    <details key={key} className="rich-details" open={node.open || undefined}>
      <summary className="rich-details-summary">
        <span className="rich-details-summary-text">{summary}</span>
        <span className="rich-details-chevron">
          <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none"
            stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="1.5" strokeLinecap="round" strokeLinejoin="round">
            <path d="M6 8L10 12L14 8" />
          </svg>
        </span>
      </summary>
      <div className="rich-details-content">{children}</div>
    </details>
  )
}

case 'spoiler':
  // SpoilerNode: inline <span class="rich-spoiler">
  return <span key={key} className="rich-spoiler" role="button" tabIndex={0}>{children}</span>
\```

**注**：SSR 下 spoiler 的 click-to-reveal 行为需浏览器端 JS hydrate，静态渲染仅输出 `rich-spoiler` class（CSS 控制遮罩）。

### A3：补全嵌套 listitem class

**文件**：`packages/rich-ssr-renderer/src/engine/renderBuiltinNode.tsx`

Lexical 序列化中嵌套 listitem 不存独立标识。检测方式：children 中包含 `list` 类型节点。但因 `renderBuiltinNode` 接收的是已渲染的 `ReactNode[]` children（非序列化节点），需在 `renderTree` 层传递额外信息。

**实际方案**：在 `renderBuiltinNode` 中检查 serialized node 的 `children` 是否包含 list 类型子节点：

\```tsx
case 'listitem': {
  const isChecklist = node.checked !== undefined
  const hasNestedList = node.children?.some(
    (c: any) => c.type === 'list'
  )
  let cls: string
  if (hasNestedList) {
    cls = 'rich-list-nested-item'
  } else if (isChecklist) {
    cls = node.checked
      ? 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-checked'
      : 'rich-list-item rich-list-item-unchecked'
  } else {
    cls = 'rich-list-item'
  }
  return <li key={key} className={cls} value={node.value}>{children}</li>
}
\```

**注**：`renderBuiltinNode` 已接收 `node`（序列化 JSON），其 `children` 字段可用于此检测。

### A4：补全 embed 样式

**文件**：`packages/rich-kit-shiro/src/style-ssr.css`

缺少 embed 样式，但默认 SSR extraNodes 包含 `embedNodes`。

\```css
@import '@haklex/rich-editor/style.css';
@import '@haklex/rich-renderers/style.css';
@import '@haklex/rich-renderer-katex/style.css';
@import '@haklex/rich-ext-code-snippet/style.css';
@import '@haklex/rich-ext-embed/style.css';       /* ← 新增 */
@import 'katex/dist/katex.min.css';
\```

---

**注**：`EditorPage.tsx:3` 和 `PresetsPage.tsx:3` 当前从 `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/ssr` 导入 `ShiroSSRRenderer`。合并后 SSR/Readonly 统一为 `ShiroRenderer`，demo 中 mode 选项可保留三态（edit/readonly/ssr）或简化为二态（edit/readonly）。
